title: Secrets, Provider Data Terms, Privacy, Retention, and Sensitive Uses · Guided Program Studio
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds governed secret and agent-data lifecycle from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of governed secret and agent-data lifecycle while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Inject secrets through approved runtime stores, scope and rotate them, prevent prompt or tool exposure, redact derivatives, and destroy test credentials.
Map purpose, legal or organizational authority, fields, sensitivity, users, provider transfer, storage, access, retention, deletion, and aggregation risk.
Record current paid, unpaid, logging, sharing, abuse-monitoring, region, age, and sensitive-use terms and re-review them before release.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Sensitive Use Review
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-sensitive-use-review-ozttnw" that uses case 5419 to enforce secret custody, data minimization, provider terms, retention, deletion, regional and sensitive-use review, and redacted evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Sensitive Use Review.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Sensitive Use Review into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Sensitive Use Review, keep Sensitive Use Review connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-sensitive-use-review-ozttnw
# Competency: agentpy-sensitive-use-review.
# Case 5419: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: loops, concurrency, tokens, time, retries, and spend stay below explicit budgets.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def evidence_agentpy_sensitive_use_review_ozttnw(data_fields: int = 28, approved_fields: int = 12, rejected_fields: int = 16, redacted_logs: int = 28, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ozttnw = "ozttnw-wnttzo-5419"
    assert evidence_variant_ozttnw
    evidence_axes_ozttnw = (
        "authorityozttnw",
        "interaction5419",
        "toolwnttzo",
        "stateozttnw5419",
        "evaluation5419ozttnw",
        "recoverywnttzo5419",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ozttnw)) == 6
    if data_fields < 1 or approved_fields < 0 or rejected_fields < 0:
        return False, data_fields, "privacy-field-count"
    if approved_fields + rejected_fields != data_fields:
        return False, approved_fields, "data-minimization"
    if redacted_logs != data_fields:
        return False, redacted_logs, "log-redaction"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert approved_fields + rejected_fields == data_fields and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, approved_fields, "governed-agent-data"
